idea.hook: How big is your cosmic home address?
facts: Our Milky Way Galaxy stretches ~100,000 light-years across.
It's one of 80+ galaxies in the Local Group, which spans ~10 million light-years.
The Local Group is just a tiny part of the Laniakea Supercluster, measuring ~520 million light-years wide and containing ~100,000 galaxies!
idea.payoff: We live in an unimaginably vast 'neighborhood' of galaxies!
idea.title: Our Galactic Home's TRUE Size! 🌌🏡🤯

scenes.text: How big is your cosmic home address?
scenes.visual: Distant, shimmering stars against a dark void, slowly drifting. A faint, swirling galaxy appears in the background.
A detailed, vibrant overhead view of the Milky Way galaxy, slowly rotating and subtly zooming out from its core.

scenes.text: 80+ galaxies form the Local Group. Spans ~10 million light-years!
scenes.visual: The Milky Way shrinks slightly as other galaxies (Andromeda, Triangulum, Magellanic Clouds) come into view, forming a loose cluster against the dark space, subtly connected by faint lines.

scenes.text: Local Group is part of Laniakea Supercluster. 100,000 galaxies, ~520 million light-years!
scenes.visual: The Local Group becomes a small dot within a vast, intricate cosmic web of countless galaxy clusters and filaments, illustrating the Laniakea Supercluster. The camera continues to zoom out, revealing the immense scale.

A breathtaking, full-screen view of the Laniakea Supercluster, shimmering with billions of stars from its myriad galaxies, slowly rotating to emphasize its immense, awe-inspiring scale. Distant cosmic dust and light fill the edges of the frame.
